projects.title: Product Catalogue with Pagination & Search
projects.description: Build a backend for an e-commerce product catalogue. Candidate must design the DB, build CRUD APIs, implement keyword search, category filtering, and cursor-based pagination. No frontend — pure REST API.

products: price INTEGER NOT NULL -- in cents

apis.description: List products — keyword search, category filter, price range, cursor pagination (20/page)
Single product detail
Create product — validate price > 0, stock >= 0
Update product fields partially
Soft delete — set deleted_at, exclude from listings
List all categories as a nested tree (parent -> children)

constraints: Cursor pagination: use id-based cursor, not offset — explain why in code comments
Search: case-insensitive match on name + description using ILIKE or tsvector
Category filter must include all sub-categories (recursive)
Price stored as INTEGER cents — never float
Deleted products return 404, not included in list results

evaluation_criteria.detail: Correct FK, indexes on category_id + price
Empty results, invalid cursor, out-of-stock filter

projects.interviewer_hint: Watch for offset pagination — it breaks with new inserts. Candidate should use WHERE id < :cursor ORDER BY id DESC. Check if they index (category_id, price) and handle recursive category lookup.

projects.title: User Cart & Checkout System
projects.description: Build a shopping cart and checkout flow. Users add/remove items, view their cart with live totals, and place an order. The checkout must deduct stock atomically — concurrent checkouts for the same product must never oversell.

apis.description: Add item to cart — if exists, increment quantity
Update quantity — quantity=0 removes item
View cart with product names, prices, line totals, grand total
Remove single item from cart
Place order — deduct stock atomically, clear cart, return order_id

constraints: Checkout must use a DB transaction with SELECT FOR UPDATE on products
If any product is out of stock, roll back entire order and return which product failed
unit_price in order_items = product price at time of checkout (not live price)
Cart total must reflect current prices — calculate on read, not stored
Adding more than available stock to cart is allowed — but checkout will fail

evaluation_criteria.detail: Calculated at read time, not stored
Clear message on stock failure with product name

projects.interviewer_hint: Key test: run two concurrent POST /checkout requests for the same item with stock=1 — only one should succeed. If candidate uses SELECT without FOR UPDATE, both will succeed (oversell). Check if they lock rows in consistent order to avoid deadlock.

projects.title: Product Review & Rating System
projects.description: Build a review system for products. Users can post one review per product (with star rating + text), edit or delete their own, and view paginated reviews per product. The product's average rating must always be accurate and fast to read.

apis.description: Submit review — one per user per product, rating 1-5 required
Edit own review — only body and rating, updates updated_at
Delete own review — recalculate product avg_rating
Paginated reviews (cursor), sorted by newest or highest rating
Distribution: { 5: 40%, 4: 30%, ... }, avg, total_count

constraints: One review per user per product — return 409 if duplicate
avg_rating on products table must update on every insert, update, delete
User can only edit/delete their own review — return 403 otherwise
Rating summary must be computed from reviews table — not cached separately
Pagination: cursor-based on review id, support sort=newest and sort=highest

evaluation_criteria.detail: UNIQUE(user_id, product_id) enforced at DB level
Cursor works with both sort orders

projects.interviewer_hint: avg_rating update: use UPDATE products SET avg_rating = (SELECT AVG(rating) FROM reviews WHERE product_id=:id), review_count = (SELECT COUNT(*) ...) inside the same transaction as the review change. Watch for candidates who only update on insert but forget delete.

projects.title: Social Media Post & Follow Feed
projects.description: Build the core of a social platform. Users post content, follow other users, and see a feed of posts from people they follow — paginated with cursors. Include like counts and a personal likes list.

apis.description: Create post — content max 280 chars
Delete own post only
Follow user — 409 if already following, 400 if self-follow
Unfollow user
Posts from followed users — cursor paginated, newest first, include author username + like_count
Like post — 409 if already liked, increment like_count
Unlike post — decrement like_count, floor at 0

constraints: Feed must use cursor pagination — return next_cursor: null at end
like_count on posts must stay in sync — update atomically with likes insert/delete
Self-follow must return 400 with clear error message
Feed should only show posts from users the current user follows — not own posts
Deleting a post must also delete all its likes (CASCADE or explicit delete)

evaluation_criteria.detail: JOIN follows -> posts correctly, no N+1

projects.interviewer_hint: Feed query: SELECT posts.* FROM posts JOIN follows ON posts.user_id = follows.following_id WHERE follows.follower_id = :me AND posts.id < :cursor ORDER BY posts.id DESC LIMIT 20. Check if candidate uses a subquery or JOIN — both fine, watch for N+1 on author name.

projects.title: Comment System with Nested Replies
projects.description: Build a comment system that supports top-level comments and one level of replies. Users can post, edit, delete their own comments. Deleted comments show placeholder text but keep replies visible. Include pagination and like counts on comments.

apis.description: Post a top-level comment
Post a reply to a comment (one level only)
Edit own comment body — update updated_at
Soft delete — set deleted_at, body becomes '[deleted]'
Paginated top-level comments with their replies nested, like_count, author username
Like comment — 409 if duplicate

constraints: Replies can only be one level deep — replying to a reply returns 400
Soft-deleted comments: return body as '[deleted]', keep author as '[deleted]', keep replies visible
Fetch all comments for a post in one query — no N+1 (assemble tree in Python)
Pagination on top-level comments only — cursor-based on comment id
like_count must stay in sync with comment_likes — update in same transaction

evaluation_criteria.detail: Single query + Python grouping, no N+1
Cursor on top-level only, replies always included

projects.interviewer_hint: Fetch all comments WHERE post_id=:id in one query. In Python, build a dict keyed by id, then group children under parents. This is faster than recursive SQL for shallow trees. Watch for candidates who do a separate query per comment — that's N+1.

projects.title: User Notifications with Preferences
projects.description: Build a notification system. The app generates notifications for events (new follower, post liked, comment replied). Users can list, read, and bulk-dismiss notifications. Users also control which notification types they want to receive via preferences.

apis.description: List notifications — cursor paginated, unread_only=true filter, include actor username
Mark single notification as read
Mark all unread notifications as read for current user
Delete a notification
Get current user's notification preferences
Update preferences — body: { type, enabled }
Internal endpoint: create notification — check prefs before inserting

constraints: Before inserting a notification, check notification_prefs — skip if enabled=false
GET /notifications must return unread_count in every response
Pagination: cursor on notification id, newest first
read-all must only mark current user's notifications — never others'
Actor username must be included in list response — use JOIN, not separate query

evaluation_criteria.detail: Notification skipped if user disabled that type
read-all scoped to current user only
Actor username fetched in same query, no N+1

projects.interviewer_hint: For read-all: UPDATE notifications SET read_at = now() WHERE user_id = :me AND read_at IS NULL — make sure candidate scopes to user_id. Watch for candidates who skip the prefs check in /internal/notify — that's the core logic of the feature.

projects.title: Personal Expense Tracker
projects.description: Build a personal finance backend. Users log income and expenses, tag them by category, and query summaries by month, category, or date range. Implement budget limits per category with an alert when spending exceeds the limit.

categories: budget_limit INTEGER -- monthly limit in cents, nullable
transactions: amount INTEGER NOT NULL -- in cents, always positive

apis.description: Log a transaction — type, amount, category, date
List with filters: type, category_id, date range — cursor paginated
Delete own transaction only
Total income, total expenses, net — for a given month or date range
Spending per category with budget_limit and % used
List categories where current month spending >= 80% of budget_limit

constraints: Amount always stored as positive INTEGER cents — type field determines direction
Summary calculations done in SQL (SUM + GROUP BY) — not in Python loops
Budget alert: calculate current month spend per category in one query
All data is per-user — never return another user's transactions
Pagination: cursor on transaction id, newest first

evaluation_criteria.detail: SUM/GROUP BY in DB, not Python

projects.interviewer_hint: Budget alert query: SELECT category_id, SUM(amount) as spent, budget_limit FROM transactions JOIN categories USING(category_id) WHERE type='expense' AND date_trunc('month', date) = date_trunc('month', now()) GROUP BY category_id, budget_limit HAVING SUM(amount) >= budget_limit * 0.8.

projects.title: Bank Account Transfer System
projects.description: Build a simple banking backend. Users have accounts with balances. They can transfer money between accounts. Every transfer creates a transaction record. Balance must never go negative and concurrent transfers must not cause double-spend.

apis.description: Create account for current user
List current user's accounts with balances
Paginated transfer history for account — cursor based
Transfer between accounts — atomic, balance check, lock rows
Transfer detail — from/to account names, amount, status

constraints: Transfer must use a DB transaction with SELECT FOR UPDATE on both accounts
Lock accounts in consistent order (lower id first) to prevent deadlock
Reject transfer if from_account balance < amount — return 422 with current balance
User can only transfer from accounts they own — 403 otherwise
Amount must be > 0 — validate before DB operations

evaluation_criteria.detail: Both debit + credit in one atomic transaction
403 if account belongs to another user

projects.interviewer_hint: Lock order matters: always SELECT FOR UPDATE WHERE id IN (:a, :b) ORDER BY id — this prevents deadlock when two transfers happen simultaneously in opposite directions. Test with concurrent requests: two transfers of full balance should result in exactly one succeeding.

projects.title: Invoice & Payment Tracker
projects.description: Build an invoicing backend for freelancers. Users create invoices with line items for clients. Invoices move through statuses (draft -> sent -> paid / overdue). Partial payments are recorded. Overdue invoices are auto-flagged.

invoices: total INTEGER -- cents, calculated from items

apis.description: Create invoice with line items — auto-calculate total
Change status draft -> sent, validate total > 0
Record payment — add to paid_amount, mark paid if fully settled
List with filters: status, client_id, overdue=true — cursor paginated
Invoice detail with line items, payments, balance_due
Total invoiced, total paid, total outstanding — for current user

constraints: total = SUM(quantity * unit_price) across invoice_items — recalculate on any item change
Status overdue: any sent invoice where due_date < today and paid_amount < total
Overpayment not allowed — reject payment if paid_amount + new_payment > total
Only invoice owner can view, edit, or record payments — 403 otherwise
balance_due = total - paid_amount — computed in query, not stored

evaluation_criteria.detail: due_date < today AND not fully paid

projects.interviewer_hint: Overdue filter in SQL: WHERE status = 'sent' AND due_date < CURRENT_DATE AND paid_amount < total. For the summary endpoint watch for candidates who do 3 separate queries — it should be one query: SELECT SUM(total), SUM(paid_amount), SUM(total - paid_amount) FROM invoices WHERE user_id = :me.

projects.title: Job Board with Applications
projects.description: Build a job board backend. Companies post jobs; candidates apply. Companies can view and update application statuses. Candidates can search jobs with filters and track their own applications. Implement pagination throughout.

apis.description: Company posts a job — role must be 'company'
Search jobs: keyword, type, location, salary range — cursor paginated
Candidate applies — 409 if already applied, 400 if job not open
Company views applicants for their job — cursor paginated with status filter
Company updates status: applied->reviewed->shortlisted->rejected->hired
Candidate views their own applications with job title + current status

constraints: Only users with role=company can POST /jobs — return 403 otherwise
Only users with role=candidate can POST /apply — return 403 otherwise
Company can only manage applications for their own jobs — 403 otherwise
Keyword search on job title + description — case-insensitive
Closed jobs (status != open) cannot receive new applications — return 400

evaluation_criteria.detail: 403 enforced for company vs candidate actions
Company only sees their own job's applications
Keyword + filters combined correctly in one query

projects.interviewer_hint: For job search with multiple optional filters, build the WHERE clause dynamically — don't write separate queries per filter combination. Watch for candidates who check role in Python but not in the DB query — both are fine, but the DB should scope data too.
